research_notes: Research conducted 2026-03-03. WKAR tracker (Feb 27, 2026) lists 25+ communities with active moratoria. Our database had 19 MI entries. This file adds 23 new community entries not in existing database, plus updates for 7 existing entries missing opposition group data. Sources: WKAR tracker, Bridge Michigan, Planet Detroit, Detroit News, Fox17, WEMU, WWMT, EDRA of MI.

new_entries.summary: Armada Township enacted a 180-day moratorium on data center development starting January 14, 2026. The moratorium gives the township time to review zoning, infrastructure capacity, and land use impacts before any data center applications can proceed.
Clark Township enacted a 1-year moratorium on data center development in June 2025, making it one of the earliest communities in Michigan to take preemptive action against data center proposals.

Dundee Township enacted a 90-day moratorium on data center development in October 2025, later extended. The moratorium freezes all data center applications including those in progress. Cloverleaf Infrastructure retracted its pre-development agreement for a data center on a 750-acre site after public outcry. At a meeting in the Dundee Village office, dozens protested with the Village president stating that people do not want it.

Grand Blanc Township Board of Trustees passed a 1-year moratorium on data center development on January 21, 2026, joining the growing list of Michigan communities pausing data center approvals while developing appropriate zoning regulations.

Hayes Township (Charlevoix County) Board of Trustees unanimously enacted a 180-day moratorium on data centers on February 10, 2026, despite no current data center proposals. After a full hour of public comment in person and over Zoom, the board struck exemption clauses that the public identified as possible loopholes. The board is drafting data center regulations for future zoning codes.

Lenox Township Board of Trustees adopted a 4-month moratorium on new data center development effective February 2, 2026, with the option for one additional 4-month extension. The moratorium gives the township time to review zoning, infrastructure capacity, utility demands, and land use impacts. The township emphasized that under Michigan law, municipalities cannot use zoning or moratoria to permanently block lawful development (exclusionary zoning).

Lodi Township (Washtenaw County) enacted a 180-day moratorium on data center development on February 3, 2026. Located near other Washtenaw County communities opposing data centers including Saline, Pittsfield Township, and York Township.

Manchester Township enacted a 2-year moratorium on data center development on October 14, 2025 -- one of the longest moratorium durations among Michigan communities. The township is located in Washtenaw County near several other communities that have also enacted moratoria.

Northville City Council unanimously approved a 12-month moratorium on data centers and similar industrial facilities on January 6, 2026, making it the first Southeast Michigan community to take proactive action before any proposals emerged. The pause allows proper planning for construction standards, environmental protections, and emergency preparedness.

Pittsfield Township trustees voted on a 180-day moratorium on any data center proposals on November 18, 2025. Supervisor Trish Reilly noted that the township's zoning ordinance does not address data centers. There are currently no known plans to build a data center in the township, but officials enacted the moratorium preemptively.

Saginaw City Council unanimously approved a resolution enacting a 6-month blanket prohibition of data centers on January 13, 2026. Council members aim to finalize a comprehensive data center policy within that timeframe to ensure responsible development that benefits the community long term.

South Lyon City Council adopted a 12-month moratorium on new data center applications on February 10, 2026, extending it beyond the original proposal of six months to match a similar pause recently enacted in Northville.

Springfield Township (Oakland County) Board of Trustees approved a 180-day moratorium on data center plans on January 7, 2026, three days after planners with consulting firm Giffels Webster told the planning commission the township was 'unprepared for the growing economic pressure to construct new data centers.' A 'long-established data center development group' is interested in an 84-acre tract in the Andersonville Road area. During the moratorium, officials will determine where data centers can be located, establish construction and cybersecurity standards, protect residential areas, and require environmental and utility studies.

Sylvan Township (near Chelsea, Washtenaw County) enacted a 6-month moratorium on data centers on January 22, 2026. Supervisor Amanda Nimke stated: 'We clearly can't stop them, but we can control them in a way that they're not going to be proposed in an area that will undermine a community.'

Taylor enacted a 1-year data center moratorium in January 2026. State Sen. Erika Geiss (D-Taylor) also introduced Senate Bills 761, 762, and 763 in December to bar data centers from withdrawing more than 2 million gallons of water a day for consumptive use and to create transparency standards for water and energy use.

Tyrone Township (Livingston County) enacted a 6-month moratorium on data center development on December 2, 2025. The township is located near Howell Township, where a major $1 billion Meta data center proposal generated significant community opposition.

The Village of Romeo enacted a data center moratorium on January 26, 2026, joining the wave of Macomb County communities (including nearby Armada Township, Lenox Township, and Sterling Heights) in pausing data center development while drafting appropriate regulations.

York Township (Washtenaw County) enacted a 6-month moratorium on data center development on February 12, 2026, after representatives for developer Sansone Group discussed a possible 1-gigawatt data center on 200 acres of property owned by Toyota at the corner of Willis and Platt roads. The proposed site is roughly 6 miles from Augusta Township's potential $1B data center, 10 miles from the Oracle/OpenAI $7B Saline Township project, and 8 miles from the UMich/Los Alamos Ypsilanti Township data center.

Solon Township (Kent County) Board unanimously approved a 6-month moratorium on new data center proposals in early February 2026, extended from an initial 90-day proposal after hearing residents' concerns. Developers are exploring farmland west of US-131, between 16 and 17 Mile roads, as a potential data center site. The property is currently zoned agricultural/residential.

Dorr Township (Allegan County) enacted a moratorium on data center development while the planning commission develops an ordinance to regulate the facilities, expected to take about six months. Microsoft purchased nearly 272 acres of land off 144th Avenue near US 131 in 2024 for more than $48 million, with 128 acres planned for data center development. Microsoft terminated its NDA with Dorr Township in late February 2026. More than 50 residents attended a December 18 board meeting raising concerns about traffic, electricity, and water impacts.
opposition_groups: Dorr Township MI - We The People

new_entries.summary: Lyon Township (Oakland County) board voted unanimously to enact a 180-day moratorium on data centers and all proposed developments 100,000+ sq ft on March 3, 2026. The moratorium followed months of community opposition to 'Project Flex,' a 1.8 million sq ft AI data center by California-based developer Verrus approved by the planning commission in September 2025 on 172 acres zoned industrial. Residents didn't learn about the project until December. The moratorium does not affect Project Flex, only newly proposed data centers.
opposition_groups: NO DATA CENTER in Lyon Township

new_entries.summary: Caledonia Township (Kent County) implemented a 6-month moratorium on data center and data mining operations effective January 1, 2026, running through June. The moratorium follows inquiries from data center developers about property at 68th Street and Kraft Avenue. The township is considering requiring special use permits for data centers to give the community more control over their applications and approval.

Erie Township (Monroe County) enacted a limited moratorium on data center development on February 10, 2026, joining nearby Dundee Township (also in Monroe County) in restricting data center proposals while developing zoning regulations.

updates.summary_addition: Big Rapids Township enacted a 1-year moratorium on data center development on February 23, 2026. An activist involved in opposition efforts got her start protesting the Gotion EV battery plant proposal outside Big Rapids.
Green Charter Township enacted a 1-year moratorium on data center development on December 9, 2025. Residents previously attempted to stop a data center via a 2026 ballot initiative.

Oshtemo Township joins Portage in formally considering a data center moratorium, with a township memo expressing concern over utility rates, water use and the environment. Residents have also organized against a Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) proposal, leading to a recall effort targeting five township board members. Opposition group founded by residents Donita and Gary DeBruin.

City of Saline implemented a 12-month moratorium on data centers on January 12, 2026. The nearby Oracle/OpenAI $7 billion Saline Township data center has generated statewide pushback. Sierra Club Michigan, Michigan Environmental Council, NRDC, and Citizens Utility Board of Michigan filed for a contested case hearing objecting to fast-track approval for DTE's contracts to serve the Saline Township data center.

Sterling Heights City Council unanimously passed a 1-year moratorium on data center development on February 4, 2026, to update a nearly five-decade-old zoning ordinance. Officials are keen on examining the impact of data centers on community resources and proximity to residential areas. The moratorium is framed not as opposition but as a measure to ensure responsible regulation.

Portage adopted a temporary moratorium ordinance pertaining to data centers and battery energy storage systems in late February 2026. The moratorium is in effect through the end of the year or until new ordinance amendments are approved. Chief Development Officer Peter Dame said the council is acting at the request of citizens who expressed concerns about the problems that can be created by such facilities.

Pontiac City Council unanimously passed a 6-month moratorium on data centers on January 21, 2026, pending further study and enactment of city regulation. Mayor Mike McGuinness said he received inquiries about data centers after his election in November, prompting proactive action. Council member Mikal Goodman has been vocal against data centers.

additional_communities_not_yet_moratoriu.note: No moratorium yet but strong opposition. Prologis proposing 312-acre '32 Mile Technical Campus' on south side of 32 Mile Road. 3+ hours of public comments at planning commission meetings. Rezoning postponed multiple times. Facebook group 'Stop Washington Township Data Center' (formerly 'Protect Washington Township - No Data Center') has 1,500+ members. Founded by resident Corinne Graper. Yard signs distributed. Prologis postponed to April 9 planning commission meeting.

No moratorium confirmed yet but strong opposition. Microsoft purchased 316 acres from Steelcase for $45.3M plus 40 additional acres for $9.7M and seeks rezoning for data center campus. Community meetings have been postponed multiple times due to overwhelming turnout. Microsoft terminated NDA with Gaines Township in late February 2026.

Ypsilanti City Council expected to vote on data center moratorium as of March 3, 2026. Two measures on agenda: a 60-day emergency ordinance from the city administration and a 365-day resolution from Ward 3 council member Amber Fellows. No data center proposals exist in the city. Community Services Director Joe Meyers calls it a preventative measure.

statewide_opposition_groups.name: Economic Development Responsibility Alliance of Michigan (EDRA of MI)
statewide_opposition_groups.description: Statewide grassroots lobbying nonprofit focused on protecting Michigan's water, farmland, and taxpayer rights. Nonpartisan and 100% volunteer. Maintains community-specific action pages and downloadable guides.

statewide_opposition_groups.name: Sierra Club Michigan Chapter
statewide_opposition_groups.description: Issued petition to Legislature to ensure Michigan's laws address hyperscale data center energy and water demands. Filed contested case hearing with Michigan Public Service Commission regarding DTE contracts for Saline Township data center.

statewide_opposition_groups.name: Stop the Data Centers - Livingston County
statewide_opposition_groups.description: Facebook group with 2,800 members opposing Meta's proposed $1B data center in Howell Township. Led by Breanne Green.

statewide_opposition_groups.name: Dorr Township MI - We The People
statewide_opposition_groups.description: Facebook group with ~500 members opposing Microsoft's proposed data center in Dorr Township. Hosted meeting at St. Mary's Catholic Visitation Parish with nearly 100 attendees. Rallying cry: 'We're going to save Pure Michigan.'

statewide_opposition_groups.name: Stop Solon Township Data Center
statewide_opposition_groups.description: Opposition group with dedicated website. Started a petition requesting 9-12 month moratorium. Concerns include proximity to 55-plus community, water usage, environmental impact, and noise.

statewide_opposition_groups.name: Stop Washington Township Data Center
statewide_opposition_groups.description: Facebook group (formerly 'Protect Washington Township - No Data Center') with 1,500+ members opposing Prologis' proposed 312-acre '32 Mile Technical Campus.' Founded by resident Corinne Graper. Has distributed yard signs. Also has a website.

statewide_opposition_groups.name: Protect Augusta Charter Township (PACT)
statewide_opposition_groups.description: Citizen group that collected nearly 1,000 signatures for a ballot referendum to reverse the township's rezoning approval for a Thor Equities data center. Also has a Facebook group 'Protect Augusta Township - NO to Data Center.'

statewide_opposition_groups.name: NO DATA CENTER in Lyon Township
statewide_opposition_groups.description: Facebook group created in December with ~2,000 members opposing Project Flex (Verrus) data center in Lyon Township.

statewide_opposition_groups.name: Michiganders Against Data Centers
statewide_opposition_groups.description: Statewide Facebook group to connect Michigan citizens opposing data center development.
